meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use SaaS pricing models to overcome a lack of portfolio. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid development.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building a high-utility SaaS pricing calculator. By productizing your logic into a deployable tool, you demonstrate technical competency and business acumen. This creates a tangible asset that proves your ability to ship, effectively replacing traditional case studies with a functional, revenue-generating demonstration.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the freelancer’s greatest adversary. You have the skills, but the lack of a formal portfolio keeps the high-ticket clients at bay. The standard advice is to 'build projects,' but without a clear focus, you end up with a graveyard of unfinished repos that do nothing to prove your commercial value.

True authority comes from solving a specific business problem. By building a sophisticated SaaS pricing model, you aren't just writing code—you are architecting a business engine. This approach shifts the conversation from 'what have you built' to 'how can you help me scale,' positioning you as a consultant rather than a commodity.

steps.title: Define the Revenue Logic
steps.description: Establish the core variables of your pricing model (CAC, LTV, Churn) to ensure the tool provides actual business value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust React component that calculates SaaS MRR and ARR based on user inputs for monthly active users and average revenue per user.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a pricing-logic.ts file with interfaces for subscription tiers'

steps.title: Architect the UI/UX
steps.description: Create a clean, professional interface that allows users to toggle between different pricing strategies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a sleek, Tailwind-styled pricing dashboard with interactive sliders for user growth projections.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement a responsive grid layout for pricing cards using Tailwind CSS'

steps.title: Integrate Data Persistence
steps.description: Enable state management so users can save their pricing configurations, showcasing your ability to handle complex data flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add local storage persistence to the pricing calculator so users can save and retrieve their custom scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a useLocalStorage hook to manage pricing state'

steps.title: Build Export Functionality
steps.description: Add a feature to export the pricing model as a PDF or CSV, demonstrating your ability to ship 'enterprise-ready' features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a function to export the current pricing dashboard data into a formatted CSV file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a button to trigger a CSV download of the current calculation'

steps.title: Optimize for Deployment
steps.description: Prepare the codebase for a one-click deploy to Vercel, ensuring your portfolio piece is live and accessible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the build configuration for optimal performance and SEO tags for the pricing tool landing page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add meta tags and configure build process for Vercel deployment'

pro_tips: Embed the tool inside your portfolio site as an iframe to show you can handle cross-domain integration.
Add a 'hidden' analytics tracking script to see how many potential clients are actually playing with your tool.
Use TypeScript interfaces to define your pricing models—it signals to senior clients that you write maintainable, scalable code.
Include a 'Compare' feature that shows how your pricing model fares against industry benchmarks like Stripe or Chargebee.
Always include a 'Contact Me to Build Your Own' CTA within the tool's interface to turn users into leads.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, share the link on LinkedIn with a breakdown of the business logic you implemented. Tag potential clients or agencies who work with SaaS companies to start conversations.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect client to find you and start building the proof they need to hire you today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
